title: Best-of discount selection applies the worst discount instead
product_summary: Acme Billing Engine - invoices with non-stacking best-of discounts (Node/Express)

ground_truth.root_cause: pickBestDiscount() in src/discounts.js sorts eligible discount candidates by savings ascending ((a, b) => a.savings - b.savings) and then takes candidates[0], so customers holding multiple codes get the SMALLEST discount; the comment above it still claims candidates are sorted best-first. The comparator must sort descending (or take the max).
ground_truth.fix_contract: With multiple eligible codes the single best discount is applied, verified on exact amounts: a 12000-cent cart with SAVE10+FLAT5 gets SAVE10 (1200 off, total 10800, '$108.00'); a 3000-cent cart with FLAT5+SAVE10 gets FLAT5 (500 off, total 2500); an 8000-cent cart with SAVE10+WELCOME15 gets WELCOME15 (1200 off, total 6800). Eligibility rules (expiry, minimum subtotal, unknown codes), the no-stacking policy, tax, and currency formatting are unchanged.
expected_evidence: discount_applied events overwhelmingly carrying the smaller savings_cents (e.g. FLAT5's 500) on accounts that also hold percentage codes
invoice_created events whose discount_code skews to FLAT5 despite SAVE10/WELCOME15 being live campaigns
distractors: the recent formatCents rewrite in src/money.js flagged in the changelog and by a customer (innocent; output is unchanged)
the stale comment in src/discounts.js claiming candidates are sorted best-first
